title: doctor --json, panoply env redesign, bootstrap hardening
outcome: panoply doctor gained --json: emits the registry object (summary + missing_defaults[] + tools[]) to stdout, suppressing all human/colored chatter via a _say wrapper; the secrets-rail check still runs silently and the exit code still reflects rail status (exit 0 here, ENFORCED). Added a human-mode 'missing defaults: …' recap line. panoply env redesigned: default prints the resolved environment (PANOPLY_PKG/MANIFEST/CONFIG/BIN, WFOS_ROOT, ONTARCH_PKG/REGISTRY, PANOPLY_HOME resolution, PANOPLY_AGENT state, manifest_version, module map of default tools); --shell prints the old activation snippet; --json emits {paths, agent, manifest_version, modules{default,alternatives}} (the modules map also feeds S5). bootstrap hardened with an explicit human-only header comment; already exits 13 under PANOPLY_AGENT=1. Schema extended with optional missing_defaults[]. Command tables in README + native-toolchain.md and the AGENTS allowed list updated.
validation: doctor --json --no-write parses via jq (total 42, installed 33, missing_default 5 = zsh-autosuggestions, zsh-syntax-highlighting, tldr, btop, dua), exit 0. env prints resolved paths + module map; env --json parses (paths+agent+modules); env --shell yields the fragment. PANOPLY_AGENT=1 bootstrap exits 13. doctor human recap line renders. Registry file rewritten with missing_defaults[] (schema-conformant). shellcheck --severity=warning CLEAN.
decisions: secrets-rail status is conveyed to --json consumers via the exit code (non-zero on drift), not embedded in the JSON, to avoid entangling the E02 rail output with the tools registry schema
env --json carries the full modules map (default + alternatives) now so S5 only needs to add doctor's replaceability section + list --matrix
kept panoply env --shell so the prior 'print the activation snippet' behavior remains available (callers / panoply env consumers)
next: E03.S3 — install plan for the 5 missing defaults (human-run install)
token_note: loaded 1 story block + STATE/ledger tail; doctor --json is a single structured read replacing per-tool probes; no canon ref needed
